faq.question: What time is it in Baghdad right now?
faq.answer: Baghdad operates on Arabia Standard Time (AST), UTC+3 year-round. The city maintains a consistent +3 hour offset from Coordinated Universal Time throughout the year, with no daylight saving time adjustments.

faq.question: What time zone is Baghdad in?
faq.answer: Baghdad uses Arabia Standard Time (AST), UTC+3. Iraq permanently adopted this timezone in 2007 and does not observe daylight saving time, unlike neighboring countries such as Jordan or Lebanon.

faq.question: Does Baghdad observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Iraq abolished daylight saving time in 2007 and has maintained Arabia Standard Time (UTC+3) year-round since then, providing consistent business hours and eliminating seasonal clock changes.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Baghdad and New York?
faq.answer: Baghdad is 8 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (November-March) and 7 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (March-November). When it's noon in New York, it's 7-8 PM in Baghdad.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Baghdad and London?
faq.answer: Baghdad is 3 hours ahead of London during Greenwich Mean Time (October-March) and 2 hours ahead during British Summer Time (March-October). The time gap narrows during UK summer months.

faq.question: What time does the stock exchange open in Baghdad?
faq.answer: The Iraq Stock Exchange opens at 10:00 AM AST and closes at 1:00 PM AST, Sunday through Thursday. Trading sessions are shorter than most global exchanges, reflecting regional business practices and the Friday-Saturday weekend.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Baghdad from the US?
faq.answer: Call Baghdad between 1:00-5:00 AM EST (9:00 AM-1:00 PM in Baghdad) to reach business hours. For evening calls, 11:00 AM-3:00 PM EST works well for connecting after 6:00 PM Baghdad time.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Baghdad?
faq.answer: Baghdad businesses typically operate 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM AST, Sunday through Thursday. Government offices often close earlier around 2:00 PM. Friday and Saturday constitute the weekend, with most commercial activity resuming Sunday morning.

content_blocks.title: Understanding Baghdad's Timezone
content_blocks.content: Baghdad operates on Arabia Standard Time (AST), maintaining UTC+3 throughout the year without daylight saving time adjustments. Iraq abolished DST in 2007, joining Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Kuwait in maintaining year-round standard time. This stability benefits international business coordination, particularly with Gulf Cooperation Council partners. The time difference with New York ranges from 7-8 hours depending on US DST observance, while London maintains a 2-3 hour gap. For precise time comparisons with major business hubs, check the time difference with New York or explore scheduling options using our meeting planner for optimal collaboration windows.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours and International Coordination
content_blocks.content: Baghdad's business week runs Sunday through Thursday, with Friday-Saturday weekends reflecting Islamic traditions shared across the Middle East. Standard office hours span 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM AST, though government ministries often close by 2:00 PM. The Iraq Stock Exchange operates 10:00 AM-1:00 PM on business days, with abbreviated sessions compared to Western markets. For European coordination, afternoon Baghdad time (2:00-5:00 PM AST) overlaps with morning London hours. US East Coast businesses can connect during Baghdad's early morning (9:00 AM-12:00 PM AST corresponds to 1:00-4:00 AM EST). Use our time difference with London calculator to identify optimal meeting windows for cross-continental collaboration.
